steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to "emphasize the order in which the supercontinents were formed." That requires the sentence to explicitly show which supercontinent came first and which came later—a clear temporal relationship between at least two supercontinents.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Forming and breaking apart over hundreds of millions of years, supercontinents are made up of most or all of Earth's continents.: Describes the general supercontinent cycle without naming either Vaalbara or Euramerica. No order is established. ✗
  • Choice B — The supercontinent Euramerica formed long after the supercontinent Vaalbara.: Names both supercontinents and uses “long after” to directly state their formation order. This is exactly what the goal requires. ✓
  • Choice C — Vaalbara formed about 3.6 billion years ago but eventually broke apart.: Gives one date for Vaalbara but never mentions Euramerica, so no relative order between the two is shown. ✗
  • Choice D — Vaalbara and Euramerica were both supercontinents, single landmasses made up of most or all of Earth's continents.: Names both supercontinents but only to define what a supercontinent is—no formation dates or relative order are given. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
